name: Fundamentals
coreQuestion: Do I understand how LLMs actually work as software components?
description: LLM API mechanics, token economics, prompt engineering, tool use, the agentic loop, structured output, context windows

levels.name: First API Call
levels.description: You can make an LLM API call and get a response
levels.checkpoint: Build a script that sends a prompt and prints the response

levels.name: Prompt Engineering
levels.description: You can use system messages, few-shot examples, and structured output
levels.checkpoint: Build a prompt that reliably extracts structured data from free text

levels.name: Tool Use
levels.description: You can define tools, handle tool calls, and manage the tool use loop
levels.checkpoint: Build an agent with 3+ tools that chains calls to solve a multi-step problem

levels.name: Agentic Loop
levels.description: You can build an autonomous agent that decides when to use tools vs. respond
levels.checkpoint: Build an agent that autonomously completes a task requiring multiple tool calls

levels.name: Production Fundamentals
levels.description: You understand token economics, context window management, and model selection
levels.checkpoint: Complete the Codebase Navigator capstone project

name: Patterns & Architecture
coreQuestion: Can I choose the right architecture for my problem?
description: ReAct, prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer, human-in-the-loop, memory patterns, multi-agent systems

levels.name: Prompt Chaining
levels.description: You can break a task into sequential LLM calls with validation gates
levels.checkpoint: Build a multi-step pipeline with at least 3 chained prompts

levels.name: Routing & Parallelization
levels.description: You can classify inputs and route to specialized handlers, running tasks in parallel
levels.checkpoint: Build a system that routes different query types to different handlers

levels.name: Orchestration
levels.description: You can build orchestrator-worker patterns and evaluator-optimizer loops
levels.checkpoint: Build a content pipeline with an orchestrator delegating to specialized workers

levels.name: Human-in-the-Loop
levels.description: You can integrate approval workflows and human feedback into agent loops
levels.checkpoint: Add approval gates and feedback incorporation to an existing agent

levels.name: Multi-Agent Systems
levels.description: You can design and coordinate multiple agents working together on complex tasks
levels.checkpoint: Complete the multi-agent capstone project

name: Tooling & Integration
coreQuestion: Can I connect my agent to the real world effectively?
description: Tool design, MCP, function calling, API integration, RAG, vector databases, external service integration, framework selection

levels.name: Basic Tool Use
levels.description: You can define tools and handle function calling with an LLM
levels.checkpoint: Build an agent with 3+ custom tools

levels.name: Framework Familiarity
levels.description: You understand the trade-offs between frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, raw SDK)
levels.checkpoint: Build the same agent with two different approaches and compare

levels.name: MCP & RAG
levels.description: You can use Model Context Protocol and implement retrieval-augmented generation
levels.checkpoint: Build an MCP server and a RAG pipeline with vector search

levels.name: Complex Integrations
levels.description: You can integrate with databases, APIs, file systems, and external services reliably
levels.checkpoint: Build an agent that orchestrates 5+ external service integrations

levels.name: Tool Architecture
levels.description: You can design tool ecosystems with versioning, discovery, and composition
levels.checkpoint: Design and implement a composable tool architecture for a production system

name: Observability
coreQuestion: Can I see what my agent is doing and why?
description: Tracing LLM calls, token usage monitoring, decision logging, conversation replay, latency tracking, cost dashboards, debugging non-deterministic behavior

levels.name: Console Logging
levels.description: You add print statements to debug your agent
levels.checkpoint: Add structured logging to an existing agent

levels.name: Trace Awareness
levels.description: You can trace a full conversation including tool calls and decisions
levels.checkpoint: Instrument an agent with tracing that captures the full decision tree

levels.name: Cost Consciousness
levels.description: You track token usage per conversation, per tool, per model
levels.checkpoint: Build a cost dashboard that tracks token usage across your agent fleet

levels.name: Anomaly Detection
levels.description: You have alerts for response quality degradation, cost spikes, and latency changes
levels.checkpoint: Set up monitoring and alerting for an agentic system

levels.name: Full Observability
levels.description: You can replay any conversation, understand any decision, explain any failure
levels.checkpoint: Instrument an existing agent with full observability and demonstrate failure analysis

name: Resilience & Reliability
coreQuestion: Does my agent handle failure gracefully?
description: Retry strategies, fallback models, graceful degradation, rate limiting, timeout handling, error recovery, circuit breakers, handling malformed tool outputs

levels.name: Basic Error Handling
levels.description: You wrap LLM calls in try/catch and return friendly error messages
levels.checkpoint: Add basic error handling to all LLM calls in an existing agent

levels.name: Retry Strategies
levels.description: You implement exponential backoff, retry with feedback, and fallback approaches
levels.checkpoint: Implement retry with feedback pattern for tool call failures

levels.name: Graceful Degradation
levels.description: Your agent continues operating with reduced capability when components fail
levels.checkpoint: Build an agent that gracefully degrades when its primary model is unavailable

levels.name: Circuit Breakers
levels.description: You have circuit breakers, rate limiting, and dynamic re-planning around failures
levels.checkpoint: Implement circuit breakers and dynamic re-planning for a multi-tool agent

levels.name: Production Resilience
levels.description: Your system handles cascading failures, maintains SLAs, and self-heals
levels.checkpoint: Design and implement a resilience strategy for a production agentic system

name: Evaluation & Testing
coreQuestion: How do I know my agent actually works?
description: Unit testing agents, LLM-as-judge evals, golden datasets, regression testing, red teaming, benchmarking, A/B testing prompts, drift detection

levels.name: Unit Testing
levels.description: You can write unit tests for deterministic parts of your agent
levels.checkpoint: Write unit tests for tool definitions and response parsing

levels.name: Eval Frameworks
levels.description: You can build evaluation suites with golden datasets and LLM-as-judge
levels.checkpoint: Create an eval suite with 20+ test cases and automated scoring

levels.name: Red Teaming
levels.description: You can systematically test your agent for failure modes and adversarial inputs
levels.checkpoint: Red team an existing agent and document 10+ failure modes

levels.name: Benchmarking
levels.description: You can benchmark agent performance and compare across model/prompt variations
levels.checkpoint: Build a benchmark suite that compares agent performance across 3+ configurations

levels.name: Continuous Evaluation
levels.description: You have automated regression testing, drift detection, and quality monitoring
levels.checkpoint: Implement continuous evaluation pipeline with drift detection

name: Security & Governance
coreQuestion: Is my agent safe to put in front of users?
description: Prompt injection defense, input/output guardrails, sandboxed execution, PII handling, approval workflows, audit trails, compliance, access control for tools

levels.name: Awareness
levels.description: You understand common attack vectors like prompt injection and data leakage
levels.checkpoint: Identify 5+ security vulnerabilities in a sample agent

levels.name: Input/Output Guardrails
levels.description: You can implement basic input validation and output filtering
levels.checkpoint: Add guardrails that prevent prompt injection and PII leakage

levels.name: Sandboxed Execution
levels.description: You can run agent actions in sandboxed environments with limited permissions
levels.checkpoint: Implement sandboxed code execution for an agent

levels.name: Audit & Compliance
levels.description: You have audit trails, approval workflows, and compliance controls
levels.checkpoint: Implement audit logging and approval workflows for sensitive actions

levels.name: Security Architecture
levels.description: You can design defense-in-depth security for production agentic systems
levels.checkpoint: Design and implement a comprehensive security architecture

name: Scale & Cost
coreQuestion: Can my agent handle production load without bankrupting me?
description: Token budgets, prompt caching, model routing, batching, async processing, deployment strategies, horizontal scaling, cost optimization

levels.name: Cost Awareness
levels.description: You understand token pricing and can estimate costs for your use case
levels.checkpoint: Calculate the per-conversation cost of your agent across different models

levels.name: Twelve-Factor Agents
levels.description: You can deploy agents following twelve-factor principles
levels.checkpoint: Deploy an agent following twelve-factor principles

levels.name: Cost Optimization
levels.description: You use prompt caching, model routing, and batching to reduce costs
levels.checkpoint: Reduce your agent's per-conversation cost by 50% through optimization

levels.name: Deployment Strategies
levels.description: You can deploy agents with blue-green deployments, canary releases, and rollbacks
levels.checkpoint: Implement a deployment pipeline with canary releases for an agentic system

levels.name: Production Scale
levels.description: You can horizontally scale agents, manage token budgets, and optimize for high throughput
levels.checkpoint: Design and implement a scaling strategy for a high-volume agentic system
